detailedDescription:
** SALE BY AUCTION - 02 July 2026 - Timed Online Auction ** EXCELLENT OPPORTUNITY to purchase a plot just over an acre of Land in PEACEFUL COUNTRYSIDE SETTING with panoramic countryside views. RARELY AVAILABLE parcel of ancient woodland. Old brown field sites, opportunity to develop an eco-home hidden within the gully subject to planning. The upper part of the woodland contains the bluebells but the old quarries are an excellent source of logs for wood burners. There is access to a water source nearby. This exceptional plot may represent an opportunity to build in a peaceful yet well-connected location subject to researching and gaining the necessary consents and usage rights. EXCELLENT LOCATION Overall, Fryarne Park offers an attractive combination of rural tranquillity, strong community spirit, beautiful countryside, and access to Canterbury, Fryarne Park) is a rural residential area on the edge of the village of Stelling Minnis, located approximately 8 miles (13 km) south of Canterbury within the beautiful Kent Downs. Historically, Fryarne Park has been associated with a medieval estate and parkland, and today it is known for its peaceful countryside setting, mature woodland, and attractive rural surroundings. The area appeals to buyers seeking a quiet village lifestyle with larger properties, open views, and access to nature while remaining within commuting distance of Canterbury and the Kent coast. The local community is one of Stelling Minnis's strongest attractions. The village has a traditional, close-knit atmosphere where residents are actively involved in local events, conservation projects, and parish activities. The large village common, one of the few surviving historic commons in Kent, acts as a focal point for community life and outdoor recreation. Residents often describe the area as friendly, safe, and family-oriented, with a strong sense of belonging that is increasingly rare in larger towns. Amenities are modest but sufficient for everyday village living. The village benefits from Stelling Minnis Post Office and Stores, which provides groceries, postal services, and daily essentials. There is also the well-known Rose and Crown pub, a popular social hub for residents. For a wider range of shopping, most residents travel to Canterbury, Ashford, or Folkestone. detailedDescription: Hope & Braim are delighted to present Honeyz at 106 Church Street in Whitby to the market. This is a rare opportunity to acquire Freehold Premises on Whitby's Prime Trading Street in the heart of the Old Town. The property is a mixed-use unit comprising a retail shop with workshop on the lower two floors and a self-contained holiday apartment on the upper two floors. The retail space is approximately 220 sq ft with double window frontage onto the busiest street for footfall in the town. Internal stairs lead down to the lower ground floor that has been converted into a workshop space and additional storage, plus there is a WC on this level. Honeyz Bath Products is a highly successful business that manufactures and sells luxury bath products from these premises since 2008 and could be included in a sale, subject to negotiation. Alternatively, the premises could be used for a new retail business. Upstairs there is a beautifully presented holiday apartment “Honeyz Hideaway” that has its own private entrance to the side of the building. On the first floor there is an open plan living space with a modern kitchen that has integrated appliances and a breakfast bar. There is a bathroom tucked under the stairs and two bedrooms on the top floor that have character with exposed beams and dormer windows. The property has been well-maintained and benefits from having gas central heating throughout.

agentCompanyAddress: Bradley Hall, 33-39 Grey Street, Newcastle Upon Tyne
detailedDescription: Bradley Hall is delighted to present Earlsway Trade Park, a prime multi-let industrial and trade park investment situated in the heart of Team Valley, the North East’s premier commercial location. Positioned prominently on Earlsway, the scheme benefits from exceptional connectivity, located directly adjacent to the A1 Western Bypass via two dedicated junctions, approximately one mile south of Gateshead town centre and three miles south of Newcastle city centre. Team Valley is widely regarded as one of the region’s most established and sought-after industrial estates, home to over 740 businesses employing in excess of 24,000 people. The property comprises a modern multi-let trade park extending to approximately 43,634 sq ft, arranged across 15 industrial and trade counter units within three separate parades. The rear terrace, constructed circa 2000, provides five units totalling approximately 21,632 sq ft, while the two front parades, developed in 2016, comprise a further nine modern units extending to approximately 22,002 sq ft. The units are of steel portal frame construction with attractive brick and profiled steel-clad elevations, benefitting from dedicated loading and parking areas. The front parades also incorporate roof-mounted solar panels, providing power generation benefits to occupiers. Earlsway Trade Park is fully occupied by a diverse mix of regional and national occupiers, providing a strong and resilient income profile. The investment currently produces a passing rent of £363,017 per annum, with opportunities for future rental growth supported by a strong occupational market and limited supply of modern industrial stock across the region. The property occupies a site area of approximately 2.70 acres (1.096 hectares) and is held part freehold and part long leasehold, with the leasehold element held on a 150-year lease from December 2018 at a peppercorn rent. Team Valley continues to experience strong occupier demand, underpinned by excellent transport infrastructure, proximity to major population centres and continued rental growth across the industrial sector. Nearby occupiers include Royal Mail, Booker, dpd, De La Rue and Express Engineering, alongside extensive retail and leisure amenities at Team Valley Retail World. Investment Summary - Prime multi-let trade park investment - Located within Team Valley, the North East’s premier industrial location - Excellent road connectivity via the A1 Western Bypass - Modern accommodation extending to 43,634 sq ft - Fully income-producing asset with passing rent of £363,017 per annum - Strong reversionary potential and asset management opportunities - Site area of approximately 2.70 acres - Offers sought in excess of £5,000,000 For further information or to arrange a viewing, please contact Bradley Hall’s commercial agency team.

summary: WELL LET FREEHOLD INVESTMENT WITH DEVELOPMENT POTENTIAL (STPC) CLOSE TO HOVE STATION
displayAddress: 90 Goldstone Villas, Hove, BN3 3RU
detailedDescription:
The property comprises the freehold interest of this attractive building, 3 flats above & a retail shop on Goldstone Villas that have been sold off by way of long leasehold interests, whilst our client has retained the freehold interest & a long leasehold interest of the garage workshop in Ethel Street which they hold for a term of 999 years from 2015. Under our clients long leasehold interest they have let the workshop for a term expiring 24 March 2031 at a rent of £14,175 per annum exclusive with a rent review 25 March 2028 & no break clauses & hold a 3 month rent deposit. There is airspace above the workshop it is thought that subject to planning consent there could be opportunity to redevelop the site or build above as per other properties in the parade. The other long lease details are as follows: GF Shop Front -£200pa-99 years from 25 March 1988, Flat 1 No GR- New 999 year lease to be created, Flat 2 -No GR- 189 years from 25 March 1992, Flat 3- No GR-125 years from 15 December 2022, Rear Garage-No GR 999 years from 18 December 2015. The property is located in Goldstone Villas & Ethel Street, Hove, adjacent to Hove Railway Station where the ground floor shop & access to the flats above are accessed from the front whilst the workshop building to the rear is accessed via Ethel Street opposite the recent Hove Gardens development which comprises a mix of residential & commercial spaces to rent. To the south of the property are both George Street & Church Road, Hove's Prime retail locations where a number of multiple operators are situated. Nearby occupiers to the property include Tesco Express, Go Botanica, Small Batch Coffee & Snap Fitness. We are instructed to invite offers in excess of £199,950 for the benefit of the freehold interest of the whole building with the benefit of the long leasehold interest of the lower ground floor workshop/ garage premises as described above . With an income of £14,175 per annum & £200 per annum ( total £14,375 PA) ground rent from the ground floor shop a purchase at the suggested level would show a net yield of 7.03% & a gross yield of 7.19%. Viewings to be arranged strictly through vendors agent.

A well-equipped kitchen is already in place, supported by multiple storage areas and facilities, providing a functional and ready-to-use environment or a blank canvas for redesign. Externally, the property benefits from a secure outdoor area with covered sections, ideal for year-round use, as well as a prominent frontage with enclosed boundaries that offer both privacy and security. summary: Former Costcutter supermarket and two flats available to purchase. Located within a pleasant market town. Freehold. Commercial section would suit a variety of alternative uses.
displayAddress: Commercial Street, Tadcaster
detailedDescription:
Former Costcutter supermarket and two flats available to purchase. Located within a pleasant market town. Freehold. Commercial section would suit a variety of alternative uses. Description - The property forms a ‘First Generation’, former Costcutter supermarket.  It is of stone construction beneath a part pitched and part flat roof. A substantial basement area is provided accessed via a rear yard. Two, separately accessed flats are also included.  There a 1 and 2-bed unit, respectively. The 1 bed flat also benefits from a pleasant external balcony. The flats have gas fired central heating, served by combination boilers. Location - The property is located in Tadcaster, a popular market town 15 miles north-east of Leeds and 10 miles south-west of York. The property is prominently positioned to the eastern side of the town on the main road extending through the centre. It benefits from a return frontage onto Rosemary Court. Tadcaster is a popular commuter town which caters for both York and Leeds.  It has seen a good deal of house price growth in the recent times. On-street parking is available to the front of the property with a maximum waiting time. A large housing estate is to the rear of the property which is beneficial from a customer perspective. Accommodation - The ground floor commercial accommodation extends to approximately 346.92 m2 (3,734 sqft).  The basement store / cellar extends to approximately 165.1 m2 (1,777 sqft).  The two flats (including communal area) extend to approximately 86.26 m2 (929 sqft). The total floor area is approximately 598.28 m2 (6,440 sqft). Tenure - We believe that the property is held on a freehold basis.  A right of way for fire escape purposes exists over the Sainsbury’s car park. The property will be sold vacant with no lease or third party agreement in place. Local Authority - North Yorkshire Council. Rates - The commercial section of the property is assessed for non-domestic rating purpose as having a rateable value of £31,000. The flats are both assessed for Council Tax Purposes as falling within Band A. Energy Performance Certificates - The commercial section has an EPC rating of D(94).  The two flats have EPC ratings of D(67) and D(68). Copies of the EPC certificates can be provided by the agent. Planning - We believe that part of the property may fall within Tadcaster’s historic Conservation Area. Planning consent for the conversion of former offices into the flats was passed in 1997 under reference CO/1997/0255. We believe that the commercial accommodation benefits from planning permission for Class E purposes. Prospective purchasers looking towards alternative uses are invited to make their own enquiries with the planning department of North Yorkshire Council. Services - We believe that all mains’ services are connected to the property. A goods lift links ground floor to basement / cellar levels.   This is presently out of order and in need of repair. The two flats benefit from gas fired central heating systems. Fire and intruder alarms are provided. Prices - Offers of around £450,000 are invited for the freehold interest. Viewings/Further Information - Please contact James Reynolds of the York office for more information or to arrange a viewing. They are intended to provide a general representation of the property and should not be relied upon as an exact depiction.
